Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.reasoning: The text on page 4 states: '289 patients (72.8%) in the combination arm... had had no PLT and were considered to have de novo metastatic disease.' 'De novo metastatic disease' is equivalent to synchronous metastases. This is also consistent with the sum of 'High volume/de novo' (214) and 'Low volume/de novo' (75) in the 'ADT Plus Docetaxel' arm in Table 1 (214+75=289).

The text on page 4 states: '...versus 286 patients (72.8%) in the ADT alone arm had had no PLT and were considered to have de novo metastatic disease.' 'De novo metastatic disease' is equivalent to synchronous metastases. This is also consistent with the sum of 'High volume/de novo' (207) and 'Low volume/de novo' (79) in the 'ADT Alone' arm in Table 1 (207+79=286).

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.reasoning: The total patients in the treatment arm were 397 (Fig 1). The text on page 4 states 289 patients (72.8%) had 'de novo metastatic disease' (synchronous). Therefore, the number of patients with metachronous metastases (prior local therapy) is 397 - 289 = 108. The percentage is (108/397)*100 = 27.2%. This is consistent with the sum of 'High volume/prior local therapy' (49) and 'Low volume/prior local therapy' (59) in the 'ADT Plus Docetaxel' arm in Table 1 (49+59=108).

The total patients in the control arm were 393 (Fig 1). The text on page 4 states 286 patients (72.8%) had 'de novo metastatic disease' (synchronous). Therefore, the number of patients with metachronous metastases (prior local therapy) is 393 - 286 = 107. The percentage is (107/393)*100 = 27.2%.
